tracks.title: Little Learners
tracks.checkpointPrompt: Finish the mission, then explain what your code is repeating or changing.

lessons.title: Meet the Turtle
lessons.description: Tell the turtle where to go and watch a line appear on the canvas.
lessons.mission: Make at least one line and add a message to your picture.
lessons.hint: Start with move(90), then try write("hello", 24).
lessons.concept: Commands run from top to bottom in order.
lessons.successMessage: Nice work. You made the turtle move and added your own message.

lessons.title: Corners and Squares
lessons.description: Use turns to make corners and close a square.
lessons.mission: Draw a square with four equal sides.
lessons.hint: A square can be made with repeat(4): move(90) turn(90).
lessons.concept: Turning the turtle changes the direction of the next line.
lessons.successMessage: Square complete. You used a loop to repeat the same two commands.

lessons.title: Repeat Magic
lessons.description: Use loops and colors to turn one shape into a pattern.
lessons.mission: Make a colorful pattern with more than one color.
lessons.hint: Try drawing a small shape, turning a little, and changing colors before you repeat again.
lessons.concept: Loops help us create patterns with less code.
lessons.starterCode:
line_width(4)
pen_color("goldenrod")
repeat(6):
    move(70)
    turn(60)

turn(20)
pen_color("teal")
repeat(6):
    move(70)
    turn(60)
lessons.successMessage: Pattern unlocked. You mixed loops and colors to build something bigger.

tracks.title: Explorer
tracks.checkpointPrompt: Build the drawing, then explain how the code structure made it easier to change.

lessons.title: Drawing with Intent
lessons.description: Connect movement commands to geometric results.
lessons.mission: Draw at least two distinct shapes on the canvas.
lessons.hint: A line plus a looped shape is enough to pass this one.
lessons.concept: Geometric output is a direct result of sequence, heading, and distance.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("coral")
line_width(5)
repeat(3):
    move(80)
    turn(120)

pen_up()
go_to(360, 220)
pen_down()
pen_color("steelblue")
repeat(4):
    move(70)
    turn(90)
lessons.successMessage: You built multiple shapes and controlled where drawing started.

lessons.title: Functions as Tools
lessons.description: Package drawing behavior into reusable helpers.
lessons.mission: Create a helper function and use it more than once.
lessons.hint: Try defining a shape helper such as def petal(): or def square(): then call it in a loop.
lessons.concept: Functions let us name and reuse a pattern of instructions.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("orchid")
line_width(3)

def petal():
    repeat(6):
        move(50)
        turn(60)

repeat(6):
    petal()
    turn(60)
lessons.successMessage: Function success. Your drawing uses a reusable helper instead of repeated copy-paste.
